Absolutely — let’s build some Session 1 discovery items for The Blight Veil that fit your campaign’s tone of witch-trial dread, decaying theocracy, and colonial corruption.

These are meant to feel tactile and charged with symbolic or narrative power — not just loot, but story catalysts. I’ll include how players might come across each item, the required skill checks (with DCs), and a short description of what the item might hint at or do later in the campaign.

docs.title: ⚜️ 1. Sanctara Prayer Coin
docs.text: Type: Religious trinket / clue Discovery: Found beneath a cracked cobblestone near the gallows or altar in Orrenval’s town square. Check: Investigation DC 13 to notice the faint circular groove in the stone that hides it. Description: A tarnished golden coin bearing the threefold eye of the Trifarix, one side rubbed smooth by years of use. Whispering a prayer while holding it makes the coin subtly vibrate. Significance: Might later serve as a key to a sanctified vault beneath the Sanctara, or react to divine corruption nearby.

docs.title: 🔮 2. Phial of Blighted Ash
docs.text: Type: Alchemical component / corruption sample Discovery: Discovered in a forgotten apothecary cabinet or on the person of a burned heretic. Check: Arcana or Medicine DC 15 to recognize the ash as not ordinary soot but the remains of something “living.” Description: A glass vial of fine, grey-black ash that shifts as if breathing. Exposure to open air causes nearby insects to die. Use: May be used in alchemical crafting or to reveal corruption — mixing it with holy water causes a violent reaction.

docs.title: 🕯️ 3. Tallow of the Condemned
docs.text: Type: Consumable / dark tool Discovery: Found in the personal belongings of an inquisitor, wrapped in linen and marked with a sigil of secrecy. Check: Religion or Arcana DC 14 to discern its ritual use. Description: A candle made from human tallow, which burns with a greenish flame and muffles sound within a 10-foot radius. Use: Grants advantage on Stealth checks in darkness for 10 minutes — but risks divine retribution (roll a d20 each use; on a 1, the nearest holy symbol cracks).

docs.title: 🕰️ 4. Broken Chronometer of Saint Varn
docs.text: Type: Relic / curiosity Discovery: Hidden in a chest belonging to a local archivist or librarian sympathetic to heretics. Check: Investigation DC 12 to locate; Arcana DC 16 to identify. Description: An ornate brass timepiece that ticks backward. If wound, it rewinds six seconds of time once per day (effect: one failed saving throw can be rerolled once/day). Lore: Saint Varn was said to have “turned back the sun” during the Burning of Orrenval — the Trifarix later erased him from scripture.

docs.title: 📜 5. Proclamation of Purity (Torn)
docs.text: Type: Historical clue / paper document Discovery: Found nailed to a half-collapsed notice board near the square. Check: History or Insight DC 10 to recall that this decree was repealed long ago. Description: A holy edict listing “approved virtues” and “signs of corruption.” The lower half is missing — possibly cut out by someone hiding the truth. Use: Clues to a forbidden sect that worshipped the Blighted Horizon; if completed later, reveals a name or sigil the Concord fears.

docs.title: 🩸 6. Iron Band of Confession
docs.text: Type: Cursed ring / relic Discovery: Found on a skeletal hand reaching from beneath chapel rubble. Check: Perception DC 13 to notice the glint; Religion DC 15 to know its purpose. Description: A ring used by inquisitors to compel confession — once worn, it tightens when its wearer lies (1d4 psychic damage per lie). Use: Can be used to interrogate others — but the wearer is equally bound by its magic.

docs.title: 🌿 7. Herbalist’s Ledger
docs.text: Type: Journal / lore item Discovery: Hidden behind a false panel in an old stall. Check: Investigation DC 14 to find; Nature DC 12 to interpret entries. Description: Lists herbs, poultices, and “forbidden tinctures of the green veins.” Marginalia mention experiments against the Blight. Use: Learning these recipes could allow players to brew minor anti-corruption salves or poisons later.

docs.title: 🪶 8. Crow-etched Pendant
docs.text: Type: Arcane talisman / omen Discovery: Found hanging from a wooden cross above a sacrificial pit. Check: Religion DC 12 to know it represents a god long erased from the pantheon. Description: A black pendant in the shape of a crow’s skull. When held, nearby crows caw in unison. Use: Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks involving omens, but prolonged use may draw attention from forbidden entities.

docs.title: Skill Check Prompts (with DC suggestions)
docs.text:
Persuasion/Deception Checks: - Convincing Inquisitor Rellis to doubt the execution: DC 14 Persuasion or Deception. - Rallying sympathetic citizens in the crowd to cause a distraction: DC 15 Persuasion.

Intimidation Checks: - Intimidating Captain Deyran into standing down temporarily: DC 17 Intimidation.

Religion/History Checks: - Interpreting the hymn scrap’s meaning: DC 13 Religion or History. - Recognizing the Cindersong sigil beneath the stage: DC 15 Religion.

Stealth/Acrobatics Checks: - Slipping through the crowd unnoticed: DC 14 Stealth. - Dodging through collapsing stalls during a chase: DC 15 Acrobatics.

Perception/Insight Checks: - Spotting the cloaked sympathizer who subtly helps them: DC 12 Perception. - Reading Rellis’s inner doubt: DC 13 Insight. - DM Exposition: "As you approach the Inquisitor, his grip tightens on his blade, and the crowd leans in, eager to see what you’ll do."

docs.title: Oct 28, 2025 | the blight veil session 1
docs.text:
Attendees: Dawson Markle Kyle Sellars Saul Zetler Paul Bridi Lo McEwan

Session 1 opens with everyone in the town square.

Cassie Thongington asks everyone who they are, purely as a plot device to get them to be bonded to each other and to explain the basics of their character to the other players.

Ashton Zennor introduces himself. A grizzly man who was a former soldier in the concord army who grew up on the rougher side of Orrenval.

Fergalous introduces themselves They are a follower of the temple of radiant dawn.

The un-named Robot introduces themselves. They have just woken up and have no knowledge of how they were powered down or how long they have been off for.

Lastly Hog introduces himself he is a scrappy dwarf and doesn't say much else.

Marla is brought onto the dias in the center of the square by captain Deyran and Inquisitor Rellis.

Marla stands accused of heresy for singing a forbidden melody and will be burned on the pyre.

The party huddles quickly to discuss their mutual disdain for the concord and decide to act swiftly. The un-named Robot has not made up their mind about how they feel about the concord yet.

The party speaks to captain Deyran and he accuses them of trying to interrupt the “trial.”

Inquisitor Rellis chimes in to say that maybe the party should be heard out and that maybe they should appeal to the desires of the people.

Deyran refuses and states that justice must be delivered by sword and by flame.

Hog suggests trial by combat and captain Deyran agrees but asks who will stand for Marla. Hog tries to suggest anyone other than him but quickly retracts that statement and suggests a 4v4.

Captain Deyran draws his sword and all the townsfolk scurry out of the square.

Combat begins.

During combat Marla is struck by an ice knife explosion and almost dies. She then tries to run away.

Inquisitor Rellis calls out for reinforcements and concord soldiers close in from almost all the exits to the square.

A shadowy figure appears from one of the alleyways and beckons Marla and the rest of the party to follow him.

Captain Deyran is killed by the unnamed robot’s Moonbeam. The party successfully escapes down the alleyway with a spell that hides their movement.

They follow the shadowy figure into a hideout. The hideout has a symbol at the center that is three rings bound together encased in flame. Hog draws a crude representation of what he assumes is the cindersong sigil.

The shadowy figure introduces himself as Vaereth. Vaereth explains that the party has drawn a lot of heat from attacking the captain in the middle of the central square.

Vaereth also explains that the party has 3 options once they leave town.

  1. The eastern gate towards the Ashroot, a large forested area where rebel groups, peaceful villagers under constant threat from the concord, and powerful shamans and druids all live.
  2. The southern gate toward the Godscar. A war ravaged land mostly devoid of civilization. LIkely a dangerous place if unprepared.
  3. The northern gate towards the coast. It is said that there is potentially a merchant town there. Not much else is known about this place.

The party briefly discusses before deciding they will travel towards the Ashroot.

They try to get more information out of Vaereth but he does not have more information to share.

Marla is taken somewhere to recover from her wounds.

The party is told it can rest in the hideout but must be gone before first light.

Before resting the party briefly talks about their aspirations.

Fergie wants to be trained by a master of fire

Unnamed Robot wants to know where their creator is and why they turned you off and how long you have been asleep for.

They long rest in the cindersong hideout

When they wake up in the morning a rumor has started in Orrenval that the party members are good for nothing scumbags and also they are extremely clumsy. No one is at the hideout any more.

They leave early in the morning around 4am. They head through the empty streets towards the eastern gate.

When they come to the eastern gate they see two guards standing on either side of the gate about 150 feet out.

They devise a plan on how to distract the guards and escape out of the city. After finding out that Unnamed robot can summon a raven. Zennor, remembering from his youth, explains that ravens are a bad omen in this city and all of them must be purged.

Unnamed robot casts a familiar spell to send a raven out to distract the guards. One of the guards leaves to track down the raven. Then Zennor climbs to the roof of a nearby building and shoots the other guard.

Combat ensues and the other guard is called back to help. The guards are both swiftly defeated.

The party exits the city but as the do they look back and see

A thin, vertical tear splits open in midair — no wider than a handspan, but impossibly deep. Inside, the world looks… wrong. Not dark, but overexposed, like staring into a reflection of stars drowning beneath an ocean.

Then, something moves within it — a silhouette. A tall, veiled figure stands just beyond the tear, visible for an instant through the light. Its face cannot be seen. Its hand presses against the barrier — almost tenderly.

A whisper fills your mind, layered and distant:

“The veil... remembers what it was made to forget.”

The fissure snaps shut. A pulse of force rolls outward — dust lifts from the marble, banners whip in all directions, and every torch within sight gutters out at once.

Then — silence again.

The Sanctara’s bells begin to toll.

Before ending the session the part members all inspect the unnamed robot and find a serial number 05C4R. They decide to call them Oscar.

docs.title: Key NPCs & Boxed DM Dialogue
docs.text:
Marla Veinwright – Defiant heretic, voice shaking but determined.

“They call it heresy, but it is only truth — a song older than their chains. Will you let them burn me for a melody?”

Inquisitor Rellis – Fanatical, but with a spark of doubt.

“The law is clear. Heresy must be purged… and yet, my heart falters. Why would the Dawnfather gift a song that tempts the innocent? Speak quickly — should I silence her?”

Captain Deyran – Brutal enforcer, eager to crush dissent.

“Enough of this dithering, Rellis! The people crave justice! By fire and blade, we show them the price of rebellion!”

Onlooker: Fearful Citizen – Terrified of Concord authority.

“Please, just let them burn her and be done with it.”

Onlooker: Sympathetic Citizen (Cindersong sympathizer) – Whispering support.

“The song she sings… I’ve heard it in my dreams. Stranger, if you value truth, do not let them take her voice.”

docs.title: Motivations & Secrets
docs.text:
• Marla: Driven by a vision of a world where truth and song are not crimes, Marla risks everything to spread the Cindersong. She hopes to inspire rebellion and awaken the spirit of resistance in others, but beneath her defiance lies deep fear for her family, who are threatened by Concord reprisals. Secretly, she wonders if her faith in the hymn is enough to save anyone.
  • Rellis: A lifelong servant of the Concord, Rellis is torn between his duty and a growing sense of doubt. He secretly keeps a forbidden hymn scrap, a relic from his childhood that reminds him of gentler times. Rellis fears that the law he enforces may be unjust, and he is haunted by dreams of the Dawnfather’s silence. He is vulnerable to persuasion, but terrified of losing his position and being branded a heretic.
  • Deyran: Ambitious and ruthless, Deyran seeks promotion and recognition within the Concord. He despises heretics and sees the Blighted as a threat to order. Beneath his bravado, Deyran hides a personal loss—his sibling was taken by the Blight, fueling his hatred and driving his need to prove himself. He is quick to violence but secretly fears that the Concord’s methods may one day turn on him.

docs.title: Environmental Details
docs.text:
• The square is paved with cracked stone, lined by looming statues of Concord heroes. The air smells of incense, sweat, and distant smoke. Vendors hawk wares nervously, and the crowd’s mood shifts with every word from the stage. Occasional gusts carry the sound of distant bells and the mutter of rumors about the Blight.
  • Weather: Overcast, with a chill wind. Occasional drizzle. The crowd huddles under cloaks and banners.
  • Crowd Behavior: Some citizens are restless, others fearful, a few defiant. Minor NPCs include a lost child, a merchant, and a Concord scribe.
  • DM Exposition: "You feel the weight of centuries in the cracked stones beneath your feet, and the statues seem to watch your every move."

docs.title: Handouts & Props
docs.text:
• Print or sketch the Scrap of Hymn, Concord Proclamation, and Cindersong Sigil for players to examine.
  • Draw a simple map of the city square and stage layout for tactical encounters and chases.

docs.title: Background Music/Sound
docs.text: • Prepare ambient crowd noise, distant bells, and tense music for dramatic moments.

docs.title: Contingency Plans
docs.text:
• If players use magic, describe how the Concord reacts (e.g., anti-magic wards, panic, or escalation).
  • If players split up, run parallel scenes and use minor NPCs to relay information or cause complications.
  • Force regrouping by triggering a major event (e.g., Concord patrols close in, forcing everyone to flee to the same alley or safehouse).
  • Use environmental hazards (collapsing stalls, spreading fire, or a sudden lockdown) to funnel the party back together.
  • Have a key NPC (e.g., Marla or a Cindersong sympathizer) send a message or create a distraction that reunites the group.
  • If players attempt legal arguments, allow checks for Law or History to sway the crowd or officials.
  • If players incite a riot, describe chaos: stalls overturned, guards overwhelmed, citizens fleeing or joining in.

docs.title: Additional Skill Checks
docs.text:
• Investigation: Searching for clues about Marla’s true motives or hidden allies (DC 13).
  • Medicine: Helping injured citizens or Marla after a rescue (DC 12).
  • Performance: Joining Marla’s song to sway the crowd (DC 15).
  • Sleight of Hand: Planting evidence or stealing keys from guards (DC 14).

docs.title: Consequences for Failure
docs.text: • If a check fails, the crowd may turn hostile, evidence may be destroyed, or the Concord may escalate force.

docs.title: Minor NPCs
docs.text:
• Lost Child: Distracts guards, may be helped or ignored.
  • Merchant: Offers information for coin, may be bribed.
  • Scribe: Records events, can be convinced to alter the official account.

docs.title: Rumors in the Crowd
docs.text:
• “The Blight Veil creeps closer each night.”
  • “Heretics sing storms awake.”
  • “The Inquisition grows desperate.”
  • DM Exposition: "You overhear a merchant mutter, ‘The Blight Veil creeps closer each night. Heretics sing storms awake.’"

docs.title: Session 1 NPC Reactions to Player Characters
Marla Veinwright Interacts with Fergie
docs.text: "You carry the fire of Emberhold, don’t you? I’ve heard the legends — the child who sparked the sky. Tell me, do you still hear the song beneath the ashlands? If you do, don’t let the Concord silence it."

docs.title: Inquisitor Rellis Interacts with Hog
docs.text: "Thunderwood, is it? I’ve read the reports. The arena, the brawls, the bottomless trousers. You bring chaos wherever you go. But tell me, Hog — do you fight for glory, or for something deeper? The Concord has little patience for legends."

docs.title: Captain Deyran Interacts with Oscar (Verdan)
docs.text: "A construct from Lumestra? I thought your kind were meant to heal, not meddle in heresy. Are you here to judge us, machine? Or do you seek to spread your roots in forbidden soil? The Concord will not tolerate unpredictable growth."

docs.title: Sympathetic Citizen (Cindersong) to Fergie
docs.text: "You’re the Emberhold smith’s child, aren’t you? My cousin saw you at the forging festival — said your flames danced with the hymn. If you stand with Marla, maybe there’s hope for us yet."

docs.title: Fearful Citizen to Hog
docs.text: "Wait, you’re Hog the Bottomless? I saw you fight in the tavern! Please, don’t start anything here — the Concord is watching."

docs.title: Marla Veinwright to Oscar (Verdan)
docs.text: "Verdancy Unit… I’ve heard of your kind. Tessaline Marr’s work, yes? If you can change, if you can choose, then maybe you’re more alive than the laws that bind us. Will you help us grow something new in this dying city?"

docs.title: Stat Blocks (Quick Reference)
docs.text:
Concord Inquisitor AC: 14 | HP: 18 | Attack: Longsword +4 (1d8+2) | Skills: Intimidation +3, Religion +2

Captain Deyran AC: 16 | HP: 28 | Attack: Greatsword +5 (2d6+3) | Skills: Athletics +4, Intimidation +5

Special Ability: Sweeping Attack (1/Encounter): Captain Deyran makes a greatsword attack against up to two adjacent targets within reach. On a hit, each target takes normal damage and must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

Marla Veinwright AC: 12 | HP: 10 | Attack: Dagger +2 (1d4+1) | Skills: Performance +4, Deception +3

docs.title: Encounter Tuning Suggestions
docs.text:
• For a large party (5+), increase Inquisitor and Captain HP by 10–20%.
  • For a small party (3 or fewer), use the listed stats.
  • For more challenge, give Captain Deyran a special ability (e.g., once per encounter, he can make a sweeping attack or intimidate the party).
  • Adjust the number of enemies for balance: 2 Inquisitors + 1 Captain is a good baseline for level 3.

docs.title: Iria Cael
docs.text: (pronounced EER-ee-uh KAYL) Alias: The Field Saint (rumor name whispered among Concord deserters) Race: Human Age: Mid 30s Alignment: Neutral Good → drifts toward True Neutral depending on how the party treats her Class (if needed mechanically): Former Cleric (Life Domain) / now an unaligned Healer Affiliation: Former Concord medic; now rogue operative loosely aligned with Cindersong sympathizers

docs.title: Appearance
docs.text:
• Lean and sun-weathered, with ropey muscle and calloused hands.
  • Her dark hair is braided close to her scalp — practical, not aesthetic — streaked with early silver.
  • She wears a bloodstained Concord medical tabard turned inside out, hiding its insignia.
  • A simple bronze sunburst charm hangs around her neck, cracked down the center.
  • Her pack is organized and utilitarian, filled with bandages, tinctures, and a small folding scalpel that gleams like it’s seen more confessions than surgeries.
  • She carries no obvious weapon — but her belt hides a short, rusty dagger etched with the phrase: “Mercy is never clean.”

docs.title: Personality & Mannerisms
docs.text:
• Voice: Calm, dry, deliberate — like someone who has learned that yelling never saves a life.
  • Demeanor: Tired compassion — still wants to help, but doesn’t believe in redemption anymore.

  • Behavioral tics:

  • Always checks her hands when nervous, as if expecting to see blood.

  • Avoids meeting eyes when lying.
  • Smells faintly of burnt herbs and antiseptic.
  • Core Impression: A woman who has performed mercy killings and never forgiven herself.

docs.title: Motivations
docs.text: Drive Description Preserve life, not faith. Once a devout medic of Lathander, she has lost faith in divine order but not in human worth. Uncover the rot in the Concord. She knows the Concord performs “purity trials” on its own wounded — experimenting with the Veil. She carries proof. Test others’ convictions. She probes everyone she meets: are you cruel because you must be, or because it’s easy? Atone without believing in forgiveness. She saves who she can, kills who she must, and expects no gods to notice.

docs.title: Behavior Toward the Party
docs.text:
• If they save the dying soldier: She recognizes mercy when she sees it — becomes wary allies.

  • “You did right by him. That’s rarer than daylight these days.”

  • If they abandon or kill him: She grows cold and skeptical.

  • “You talk about justice, but all I see are new uniforms for old sins.”

  • If Fergie reveals his faith:

  • “Another Dawn-priest? Then you already know what light does when it burns too long.”

  • If Oscar speaks of the Concord:

  • “You’re their reflection, metal one. I hope you like what you see.”

docs.title: Initial Encounter (Roadside Scene)
docs.text:
“If you’ve come to finish the job, at least let him die clean.” “I was a medic once. Concord field division. That was before I realized we were stitching soldiers back together just so the pyres had someone new to burn.”

If the party heals or comforts the soldier:

“You’re not like the rest. Maybe you’ve still got something worth saving.”

If they kill him:

“Mercy’s faster, at least. But don’t lie to yourself — you did it for you, not him.”

docs.title: Campfire Reflection (if she travels with them)
docs.text: “Do you ever wonder what comes after the Veil? Or do you just pray it never opens again?” “You can tell a lot about a person by who they decide is worth saving.” “I patched up enough zealots to know the holy light burns hottest on those who’ve never stood in shadow.”

docs.title: Revelation Scene (later encounter)
docs.text:
If the party gains her trust, she reveals she carries a sealed Concord dossier — medical orders signed by Inquisitor Rellis authorizing the use of Veil fractures on “noncompliant test subjects.”

“I saw what they did in the infirmary beneath the Sanctara. They called it purification. The Veil didn’t take them — it remembered them.”

docs.title: Secret Background
docs.text:
• Past: Iria served in the Third Sun Division, a Concord field medic unit stationed along the Godscar. When the blight began infecting soldiers, she was ordered to experiment with “Veil exposure.” The results were catastrophic — bodies turned hollow, minds unthreaded. When she refused to continue, the Inquisitors marked her as a deserter.

  • Present: She’s been moving between Cindersong contacts, trading stolen medical knowledge for protection. She doesn’t fully trust Vaereth — suspects Cindersong’s motives aren’t as pure as they claim.

  • Secret: Iria is Veil-touched. During her experiments, a tear opened and marked her.

  • She occasionally hears echoes of the dead she’s treated.

  • Her blood glows faintly in moonlight.
  • She dreams of a burning ward filled with singing patients. She hides these symptoms, believing they’re penance, not power.

docs.title: Mechanical Notes (if you need stats)
docs.text:
• AC: 12 (unarmored)
  • HP: 27 (5d8 + 5)
  • Speed: 30 ft
  • Attacks: Dagger (+4 to hit, 1d4+2 damage)
  • Spells (once cleric): Spare the Dying, Cure Wounds, Detect Poison and Disease, Calm Emotions

  • Special:

  • Veil Echo (1/day): When she stabilizes a dying creature, they experience a fleeting vision of the Veil — roll a d6; on a 1, they gain 1 level of exhaustion.

  • Unholy Insight: Advantage on Medicine checks to diagnose blight-related ailments.

docs.title: DM Hooks
docs.text:
• She could become a temporary traveling companion — guiding them through Ashroot in exchange for safety.
  • She might later be captured by Concord and reappear in a moral dilemma rescue.
  • If she dies, her blood briefly opens a flicker of the Veil — revealing the same silhouette from Session 1.
  • If she survives long-term, she could become a moral foil to Fergie, or a reluctant confidante for Oscar.

docs.title: Session 2 Ideas
docs.text: Based on the possible outcomes and choices from Session 1, here are prompts and ideas for Session 2. Each path leads to a unique scenario, allowing you to build on the consequences of the players' actions.

docs.title: If the Players Intervened Publicly (Confrontation)
docs.text:
• Prompt: The party has fled with Marla into the alleys, pursued by Concord patrols. The city is on high alert, and wanted posters bearing their likenesses appear overnight. The Cindersong offers sanctuary, but demands a favor in return: sabotage a Concord ritual or rescue another sympathizer from captivity.
  • Session 2 Ideas:
  • Urban chase and stealth scenes as the party evades capture.
  • Planning and executing a sabotage or rescue mission for the Cindersong.
  • Navigating a city under lockdown, dealing with informants, and making alliances with other rebels or outcasts.

docs.title: If the Players Negotiated for Marla’s Release
docs.text:
• Prompt: The party is marked as suspect but not openly hunted. The Concord watches their every move, and the Faith questions their motives. Marla, now in their custody, reveals secrets about the Cindersong and the true nature of the hymn.
  • Session 2 Ideas:
  • Investigating the origins and power of the Cindersong hymn.
  • Dealing with Concord surveillance and subtle threats from the Faith.
  • Deciding whether to help Marla spread her message or keep her hidden.

docs.title: If the Players Rescued Marla via Stealth/Deception
docs.text:
• Prompt: The party reaches a safehouse, but Concord agents and rival factions are searching for them. The Cindersong recognizes their skill and asks them to infiltrate a Concord stronghold to retrieve a vital artifact or document.
  • Session 2 Ideas:
  • Planning and executing a heist or infiltration mission.
  • Interacting with Cindersong contacts and learning more about their network.
  • Facing moral dilemmas about what to do with the information or artifact they recover.

docs.title: If the Players Did Nothing / Observed
docs.text:
• Prompt: The party is marked as witnesses and potential recruits by the Cindersong, but also as suspicious by the Concord. They receive a coded message inviting them to the catacombs, where a secret meeting is held.
  • Session 2 Ideas:
  • Attending a clandestine gathering in the catacombs, meeting key Cindersong figures.
  • Being offered a choice: join the resistance, inform on them, or walk away.
  • Uncovering new rumors and secrets about the Blight Veil and the city’s underbelly.

docs.title: General Session 2 Hooks
docs.text:
• The city remains tense, with rumors of rebellion and increased Concord patrols.
  • NPCs from Session 1 (Marla, Rellis, Deyran, Cindersong sympathizers) reappear with new motives and information.
  • The party’s reputation and choices affect how they are treated by various factions.
  • New opportunities arise for alliances, betrayals, and uncovering deeper mysteries about the Blight Veil and the Concord’s secrets.

Use these prompts to tailor Session 2 to your players’ choices, ensuring that their actions have meaningful consequences and open up new storylines.
